titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Mary Smyth Hunter Correspondence
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12405801.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 28, 2018 at 19:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to Mary Hunter, Phyllis Williamson and Elizabeth Williamson from Ethel Smyth, Edith Wharton, Virginia Woolf and others, with related papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I. consists of letters to Mary Smyth Hunter. Larger files can be found for Henry James, Claude Monet, George Moore, Jogn Singer Sargent, and Ethel Smyth. Other correspondents include Max Beerbohm, Gabriel Fauré, Edmund Gosse, Edvard Grieg, George Henschel, William James, Auguste Rodin, and Edith Wharton.

did.unittitle: Louise Margaret, Princess, Duchess of Connaught
